filename: Lifestyle case study female BodyFitness competitor.pdf
source: Lifestyle case study female BodyFitness competitor.pdf
strategy: agentic chunking via Gemini (headline + summary + original text)

pages.text:
BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2014

Personal Training Client Application Form

The more information you provide, the better I will be able to assist you. If you have
any questions with regards to this form, please do not hesitate to contact me. You
can use whichever measurement system (e.g. metric, imperial) you prefer to fill in
this form. Just state which unit you used (e.g. cm, kg, lb, inch).

 Full legal name: M
 Full billing address:
 Age:33
 Height:5"4
 Weight: 62.9 KG on SATURDAY 64.3 Tuesday. Refeed was Sunday evening.
 Body fat percentage (plus measurement technique): Not had measured.
 Years of training experience: 1.9 years (weights based).
For the section below, please give your current maximum abilities in weight times
reps, e.g. 250 lb x 6.
 Bench press: 20kg x 15 (don't go heavy, so hard to say)
 Squat (excluding body weight): Usually 40kg
 Chin-up (excluding body weight): Don't do!
 Overhead press: 20/25/kg x15 (as bench press - don't go heavy)

What are your fitness goals?
Competition - body fitness figure BUT nearer bikini than physique at threshold.
Photo shoots
To maintain a shape similar stage look year round (obviously not that lean)
Break my current fat loss plateau

How often in a week would you be prepared to train, if this was the optimal training
frequency?
X6

What is your occupation? You may be general here, e.g. student/manager/personal
trainer/manual labor/doctor. The aim is to characterize your circadian rhythm,
stress and activity level.
Head of Department for English: Secondary School teaching
This is VERY stressful, and stress is at a maximum this year due to inspection
pressure.

Please list any pathologies and injuries (disabilities, allergies, illnesses, syndromes,
disorders etc.) you have or had:
None aware of

BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2014

Do you plan to use any PEDs while under my supervision? If so, please list them:
What is this?

My coach had me on anavar 5 mg and clenbuterol 160 mcg per day, if that's what you
mean. I'd prefer not to take this, but I will do what's best

Note that if you answer 'NO' to any of the questions below, I will not be able to take
you on as a client.
 I declare that, while Menno Henselmans is my personal trainer, all the
substances I use are obtained and used in accordance with their governing
laws: YES
 I forego all rights to hold Menno Henselmans liable for any damages caused in
relation to following his advice as my trainer: YES

At this point, you may choose to return this form to me to obtain a price
quote before filling in the rest. Please return the filled in form to
mennohenselmans@bayesianbodybuilding.com and attach at least two full-body
pictures of yourself in clear lighting, preferably including a back double biceps and
front relaxed pose.

Activity level: please mark one of the fields below or delete the rest.
 Active (e.g. on your feet much of the day)
Stress level: please mark one of the fields below or delete the rest.
 High stress (e.g. very high-paced work environment with great responsibility)
If you answered yes to the question on PED usage, you can leave the section below
blank.
 Wrist circumference:6.25"
 Ankle circumference: 6.25"
 How much did you weigh before you started lifting and what was your height
at that point? Same height, 64.6KG
 Were you heavy as a baby? No
 Please attach a picture to this form in which the back of your right hand is
clearly visible. Ideally, place your right hand on a flat surface with your fingers
touching each other and then take a picture of it. Emailed separately.
headings: At this point, you may choose to return this form to me to obtain a price
quote before filling in the rest. Please return the filled in form to

pages.text:
BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2014

What are the lightest barbell weight plates you have access to? 2.5 KG - but lightest
bar bell is 10kg

What are the smallest weight increments between the sets of dumbbells you have
access to?
2.5, 4, 5, 6, 7.5, 10 - bigger jumps from then on I think.

Do you have access to…
a squat cage or rack? Yes
a 45° hyperextension bench? Yes
a reverse hyper machine? No
a glute-ham raise? Seated Leg curl machine/swiss ball
a standing calf raise machine? Yes
a dip/chin-up belt? Not sure - assisted pull up and pull up frame
a leg curl machine and if so, is it seated, lying or standing?
a pair of knee wraps? ?
gymnastic rings or a TRX? No
powerlifting bands or chains? No
a seated calf raise where you push your body up (closed chain: see example image
below)? Yes, but not very good!

Please list all the supplements you are currently taking:
BCAA drink
Multi Vit
HMB
L Glutamine
Cod liver oil tab
Please describe or attach your current diet plan in detail:
Each day:
Coconut oil tsp
M1: 100g Steak mince, 15g PB
M2: 85g Chicken, ½ avocado
M3: 100g Salmon, 6 asparagus spears, 15g PB
M4: 85g chicken, 6 asparagus, 1tbsp olive oil

BAYESIAN BODYBUILDING 2014

M5: x2 whole eggs, 6 asparagus spears, 15g PB
Post work out, carb/fat free whey 30g

Please describe or attach your current training program in detail:
X6 weights each group
X6 cardio x90 mins - varied

Are there any times at which you are unable or unwilling to train? If so, please list
them:
Usually x6 days - sometimes will be x5 depending on child care arrangements. Other
than that will train all year.
Ideally 5

Please return the filled in form to mennohenselmans@bayesianbodybuilding.com
and attach at least two full-body pictures of yourself in clear lighting (without
excessive shadows), preferably including a back double biceps and front relaxed
pose. If you recently had blood work done, you can attach its results as well.

This is an effectively novice level trainee on steroids (the clenbuterol dosage is high
even for pro bodybuilding standards!) eating a ketogenic, very low calorie diet and
training 18 hours a week without making any progress. What gives? Extremely high
chronic stress and sleep deprivation. She got up in morning (night) to do 90 minutes
of cardio before going to work. She worked until late and afterwards she did her
strength training. After her post-workout meal it was usually midnight.

So here's what we did. We raised her calories to just over 2100 and increased her
carbs above the ketogenic threshol. We dropped all cardio and took her off the
ludicrous amount of clen. My goal was to reestablish weight maintenance, destress
her and let her recovery from potential overtraining before continuing her cut.

The result: She actually started rapidly losing fat while staying the same weight (close
to perfect body recomposition) and she is currently squatting with 100 kg after less
than a month. (Note that she's still on AAS, even if the dosage is weaker than what a
male naturally produces.) She also underwent a personality change from being just
short of a mental breakdown to being very happy.

Take home message: do not underestimate lifestyle factors.
